title: Farmhouse Console Table (46" Width, Caster Option)
summary: A two-leg-set 2x4 console with scribed X-brace sides and an optional caster base, sized short enough to work as an outdoor grill stand or standard-height sofa table.
description:
This console builds on just two leg sets rather than the three-leg trestle base used elsewhere in this catalog, joined lengthwise by 2x6 supports and slatted bottom shelving — a simpler, faster structure for a table this width. The scribed X-brace on each side is worth doing before the sides are attached to the rest of the frame: hold the 2x2 in place against the leg opening, trace the actual cut lines with a pencil rather than trusting a fixed measurement, then cut and attach.

**Leg length is genuinely a choice here, not a fixed spec** — build 28.5" legs (with no casters) for a standard 30" tabletop height, or 24.5" legs with 2" casters for a shorter ~27" counter-height table, which the source plan notes was sized specifically to match a griddle or grill top's working height. Decide which you want before cutting, since the two options aren't interchangeable after the fact.

The bottom shelf slats go on with a small 1/8" gap between boards, and the top is two ripped 2x10 boards attached from underneath with no visible top-side fasteners. If you choose the caster option, thread the casters on last, after the base is fully assembled and turned right-side up.

materials.name: Pocket hole screws, 2-1/2", or self-tapping wood screws
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Cut to fit; scribe in place. Outside angles about 34°, inside crossing angle about 24°.

steps.title: Build the leg sets
steps.body: Attach the legs to the horizontal 2x6 leg supports. Use 2-1/2" self-tapping screws, or drill 1-1/2" pocket holes on the 2x6 ends and attach with 2-1/2" pocket hole screws and glue. Also drill pocket holes for the apron attachment in the next step. Build two identical leg sets.
materials: Pocket hole screws, 2-1/2", or self-tapping wood screws

steps.title: Scribe and add the X braces
steps.body: With the sides not yet attached to the rest of the frame, hold the 2x2 braces in place, trace the actual cut lines with a pencil, then cut and attach with screws or brad nails and glue.
materials: Pocket hole screws, 2-1/2", or self-tapping wood screws

steps.title: Attach the aprons
steps.body: Attach the 2x2 aprons to the 2x6 leg supports, joining the two leg sets into the full base frame.
materials: Pocket hole screws, 2-1/2", or self-tapping wood screws

steps.title: Add the bottom shelf slats
steps.body: Attach the bottom shelf slats with 1-1/2" pocket holes and 2-1/2" pocket hole screws, spacing about 1/8" between slats.
materials: Pocket hole screws, 2-1/2", or self-tapping wood screws

steps.title: Attach the top
steps.body: Rip the 2x10 boards to 9.25" and attach the top boards from underneath with 2-1/2" screws and glue, keeping overhangs even.
materials: Pocket hole screws, 2-1/2", or self-tapping wood screws

steps.title: Add casters (optional)
steps.body: With the base assembled and right-side up, attach casters to the bottom of each leg.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

images.alt: 46-inch farmhouse console table with X-braced sides
